title: Your Life at Every Rank in North Korea (You Can't Leave)
hook: You're born into a caste you didn't choose, in a country where the ration runs out before the month does. Climb every real rank of the system and you'll finally understand: the top isn't freedom. It's just a better-fed version of the same cage.
body: POV: you're a North Korean citizen climbing the country's real, documented power ladder — songbun caste, the Party, the capital, and the family above all of it. THE FARM: songbun sorts you into a caste at birth; the official ration target is 700 grams of grain a day — some months, it's zero. THE CANDIDATE: Korean Workers' Party membership, roughly 3 million of 25 million people, earned partly through your own neighborhood's informant network. THE CADRE: a full Party post, an official wage of about $30 a month — the black-market jangmadang quietly pays the rest. PYONGYANG: only the most loyal top 10% of the country may legally live in the capital at all. THE CENTRAL COMMITTEE: 235 seats — and the real, documented '연좌제' three-generations rule, where one relative's political offense can send an entire family, grandchildren included, to a kwanliso political prison camp (an estimated 80,000-120,000 people currently held, confirmed by satellite imagery). THE POLITBURO: roughly 19 full members and 11 alternates, watched by the state as closely as anyone below them. THE COURT: the Leader's inner circle, marked by the rarest of North Korea's 20+ real portrait badges — worn, by design, by only a few hundred people alive. THE APEX: Office 39, the real hard-currency operation that funds the ruling family, estimated at $500 million to $1 billion a year. And the one thing every rank in this system has in common: nobody, at any level, is free to simply leave. A dramatized cautionary POV — the narrator is a fictional composite; the documented mechanics of the system are real, sourced, and never a how-to. Watch the whole series — every life, every rank, every way the climb costs you. For educational and entertainment purposes.
pinned: Here's the part that stays with me: the real 연좌제 law means one relative's political offense — sometimes decades old — can send three generations of a family to a prison camp together, grandchildren included. Which rank do you think a person can never actually come back from?
poll: Which level costs the most? THE FARM (the ration that runs out) / THE CANDIDATE (informing on a neighbor) / THE CENTRAL COMMITTEE (the three-generations rule) / THE APEX (you still can't leave)
